LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CLOVIS SILVA'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND ALL REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATIONS AND REASONS FOR THOSE RECOMMENDATIONS IN THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CLOVIS SILVA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED FOR TWO REASONS. FIRST, THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY. ALTHOUGH THE PARTIES DISAGREE WHETHER THE MOTION WAS DUE ON NOVEMBER 5 OR 6, 2018, IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT ON NOVEMBER 5, 2018, PLAINTIFF FILED ONLY A NOTICE OF MOTION, AND THAT THE BALANCE OF THE PAPERS, INCLUDING THE SUPPORTING DECLARATIONS, WERE NOT FILED UNTIL NOVEMBER 8, 2018. THAT RENDERS THE MOTION UNTIMELY. CAL. CODE CIV. PROC. 1008(A); BRANNER V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, 175 CAL. APP. 4TH 1043, 1045 (2009). SECOND, EVEN IF THE MOTION WAS TIMELY, IT DOES NOT SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR RECONSIDERATION. THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION MUST SHOW "WHAT APPLICATION WAS MADE BEFORE, WHEN AND TO WHAT JUDGE, WHAT ORDER OR DECISIONS WERE MADE, AND WHAT NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW ARE CLAIMED TO BE SHOWN. CAL. CODE CIV. PROC. 1008(A). PLAINTIFF PREVIOUSLY ADVANCED THE SAME ARGUMENT WHY THE SUBPOENAS SHOULD BE QUASHED AS HE DOES HERE, AND HE DOES NOT SATISFACTORILY ESTABLISH WHY HE COULD NOT HAVE INTRODUCED THE SAN DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF HIS ORIGINAL MOTION TO QUASH THE SUBPOENAS. MOTIONS FOR RECONSIDERATION ARE APPROPRIATE ONLY IN EXTRAORDINARY CIRCUMSTANCES. NEW YORK TIMES CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT, 135 CAL.APP.4TH 206, 212-13 (2005). THIS IS NOT ONE OF THEM. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: JEFFREY WOHL, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-21-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying In Part Plaintiff'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CLOVIS SILVA's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying In Part Plaintiff'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas.

The motion for reconsideration is denied for two reasons.

First, the motion is untimely. Although the parties disagree whether the motion was due on November 5 or 6, 2018, it is undisputed that on November 5, 2018, plaintiff filed only a notice of motion, and that the balance of the papers, including the supporting declarations, were not filed until November 8, 2018. That renders the motion untimely. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 1008(a); Branner v. Regents of University of California, 175 Cal. App. 4th 1043, 1045 (2009).

Second, even if the motion was timely, it does not show good cause for reconsideration. The supporting declaration must show "what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 1008(a). Plaintiff previously advanced the same argument why the subpoenas should be quashed as he does here, and he does not satisfactorily establish why he could not have introduced the San declaration in support of his original motion to quash the subpoenas. Motions for reconsideration are appropriate only in extraordinary circumstances. New York Times Co. v. Superior Court, 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212-13 (2005). This is not one of them.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey Wohl, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas; And For A Protective Order; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Boone Callaway, Esq. 415-541-0300 of Callaway & Wolf for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Plainitff informs the Court that no one is contesting the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CLOVIS SILVA'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas; And For A Protective Order; And For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff Clovis Silva's motion to quash subpoenas and for a protective order is granted in part and all requests for monetary sanctions are denied. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
